seoTitle: How to Integrate Jira With Microsoft Outlook
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Jira with Microsoft Outlook, create issues directly from emails, and link conversations to existing tickets.
intro: Switching between your inbox and Jira to log every request slows you down. This guide covers integrating Jira with Microsoft Outlook so you can create issues from emails and link conversations to tickets without leaving your inbox.

steps.heading: Review the integration overview
steps.body: Understand what the Outlook integration enables — namely, creating and linking Jira issues directly from your email client.

steps.heading: Connect Jira to Outlook
steps.body: Install the integration and authenticate your Jira account from within Outlook.

steps.heading: Create issues from emails
steps.body: Use the integration's toolbar option to turn an email directly into a new Jira issue without retyping the details.

steps.heading: Link emails and manage workflow
steps.body: Attach existing emails to related Jira tickets so context stays connected between your inbox and your board.

faq.question: How do I integrate Jira with Microsoft Outlook?
faq.answer: Install the Jira for Outlook add-in, connect your Jira account, and use the toolbar to create or link issues from within Outlook.

faq.question: Can I create a Jira issue directly from an email?
faq.answer: Yes, once the integration is set up, you can turn an email into a new Jira issue without switching applications.

faq.question: Can I link an existing email to a Jira ticket I already created?
faq.answer: Yes, the integration lets you attach relevant emails to existing tickets to keep context together.

faq.question: Who benefits most from the Jira Outlook integration?
faq.answer: Support teams, project managers, and anyone who receives requests by email benefits from being able to turn messages into tracked tickets quickly.
